transcript: Welcome to the 17th FIFA World Cup Final, featuring Germany for a seventh time, featuring Brazil for a seventh time, but it's the first ever meeting between these footballing superpowers on the World Cup stage and comes here at the International Stadium in Yokohama. So here we go, the conclusion to a captivating month, which has seen plenty of shocks along the way, but ends with two perennial contenders going toe-to-toe. Both Germany and Brazil had uncharacteristic issues even qualifying for the competition but their quality endures and we have a fitting final Brazil free kick is launched long Germany trying to equal Brazil's record of four World Cup wins here's Neville away from Roque Junior clipped by Gilberto Silva Germany able to advance on we go but Schneider getting it back here and delivering the cross with closer calling forward in the middle it needed the important intervention of Ed Milson there to divert it away from danger just got to it I think ahead of his goalkeeper Marcos early attacking endeavour coming from Rudi Völler's German side Pierluigi Kalina no place to adjudicate on that lets it go with Brazil still on the ball Ronaldinho slipping it through Ronaldo's in and it's wide and in the context of his campaign fairly believably wide from Ronaldo who would surely have expected to open the scoring here can't out quickly a first direct confrontation between the two and a battle that has been given top billing in the build-up. Scolari says whatever happens he'll be unemployed tomorrow at the end of his contract. Ronaldinho, beautiful ball over the top for Ronaldo who couldn't quite get his shot away. Kahn again out quickly and again gets the better of the duel with Ronaldo. Just couldn't quite connect here. He's all looking a threat on the counter-attack. so far still only Robbie Keane of the Republic of Ireland has scored past Oliver Kahn against Germany at this World Cup Thorsten Frings bird of Bremen Nielsen with him can't prevent the cross from coming in just too high for closer though unable to stretch to meet it but naville will keep the danger alive here in retrieving squaring up to the brazilian captain cafu and teeing up james jeremy's space here to have a strike it needed to be spectacular it was ambitious and inviting but why did the target it goes but the man he's replaced would have fared better the suspended Michael Ballack scoring winners of course in the last two rounds but restricted to a watching brief tonight Firinho dropping a little deeper Cleverson who's on the charge here and Cleverson is able to get his shot away despite the presence of Metzelda just wide a rising star of this team who started the tournament on the bench he's since displaced Janinho from the starting line-up and Albinio set out for semi-final after he's sending off against England of course Cleverson prompting and probing, back to Ronaldinho once more Cleverson he's gonna have, so unlucky, corrects it against the crossbar, with Kahn beaten by that, really good hit from Cleverson, who only made his debut for Brazil in February, really making an impact, the role of Rudi Bolles' side beginning to lead a charmed life here. Going crossfield, Ronaldinho, to Roberto Carlos, the try and flash one in, it will find the feet of Ronaldo! Who usually from there finds the back of the net instead it's saved by the legs of Oliver Kahn the last action frantic last action of the first half a hat-trick of presentable chances for Ronaldo in this first half and he's not been able to take any of them Kahn again was in the way to deny Brazil's nine and the final remains deadlocked at nil-nil Steve Mahama over to Nobile with a little nick actually it's going to carry for the corner Germany starting the second half on front foot, not that they appear too concerned at this juncture. A bit of movement in the middle, awaiting this corner kick, very congested penalty area. Oh but it hasn't prevented Jens Jeremies finding the space there to get the header away, the unlikely of Jeremies close just deflected away I think Bayard Mielsen here yeah he's the one that flung out that leg to divert the danger and he felt Voller's men would just sit back and defend tonight but not a bit of it Schneider going down to win the free kick again under the nose ofina, Scolari's men been pinned back a bit since the restart, this will certainly have to be spectacular from here, Ovil winding up though to have a hit, oh and it was certainly spectacular, a stunning swerving strike and an equally fine save from Marcos to foil him and just touch that on to the post brilliant effort wonderful stop reveal the man who got their late winner in the round of 16 against Paraguay so close here to opening the scoring in the final Scolari with plenty to thank his goalkeeper for having won a Copa Libertadores together at Palmeiras That's Costa Frings Oswil Enric Capu so finely balanced at the moment this final this is Marco Moda infield to Didi Havane to strike one. Plenty of power but not the requisite accuracy this time from the Liverpool player. Marco's watching it over. It's becoming a familiar theme just at the moment. Thorsten Frenz. Bert Schneider. familiar theme just at the moment Boston friends Bernschneider the most likely maybe creatively to make something happen for Germany and he's almost slipped it there into the path of his Leverkusen teammate Merville who just couldn't quite connect will they be the nearly men again four Leverkusen players in the Germany squad plus Leccio of Brazil Ronaldo position of some promise down goes Ronaldo back to his feet here to win the ball back and dispossess Hamann now Rivaldo oh and Kahn spilled it, Ronaldo, to make the breakthrough, his predatory instance take over, and Brazil lead midway through this second half, well you have to feel for Oliver Kahn, who's had an excellent World Cup, but he's left to reflect there on the one that got away, couldn't hold Rdo's effort and Ronaldo following up sports his 7th goal of this World Cup 4 years on from the final heartache against France and all the injury issues that have followed Ronaldo is back Brazil's first goal in a World Cup final since 1970 the last time any country won every game outright on route to this title, Scamari's side on course to emulate them brings forward for the last 20 minutes now in Yokohama Schmeider will look to take the throw quickly Germany having to show even more urgency now here's Jeremies so he's wriggled into a little bit of room to create a glimpse of goal it's all it was reflected just wide of the target from Jerem left it Ronaldo scores for the second time in the space of a dozen second half minutes and Brazil well on their way to a record fifth World Cup success a beautiful Brazilian goal Cleverson was strong, Rivaldo was aware and Ronaldo was precise redemption and retribution for Ronaldo a striker back at the very summit of the World Cup with another swipe of that golden boot a long way back from here for Germany Gerald Asamoah only just on before that second goal went in his cross is clear this is link up to Prings to Oliver Bierhoff smart shot on the turn equally fine save from the impressive Marcos, Mesut Bierhoff who forged his reputation with two goals in the Euro 96 final now out to launch a salvage job at the other end of his international career Brazil here waiting to bring on Janinho going into the final five minutes Thomas Linker forward Schneider goes down and will win the free kick never really for the Germans in goes that free kick it's missed out everybody in the middle but it's also missed by Christoph Metzelder at the far post just failed to connect when really well placed how costly Danielson the foul from Asamoah but there'll be no time to take the free kick as the full-time whistle goes and Brazil are champions of the world once more a record-extending fifth title comes at the expense of Germany who suffered defeat in the final for the fourth time well both teams it may be exceeded initial expectations in getting this far this year but it's those two goals from Ronaldo that prove the difference on the night Cafu's already made history this evening by appearing in a third successive World Cup final. It's the second one he's won, and this time he lifts the trophy as captain. The curtain comes down on Asia's first World Cup, and it does so with familiar scenes of samba celebrations. Brazil had to settle for the runners-up spot in France four years ago, but they're now back on the very top step of the global game. World champions again in 2002.
